mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered months, or the signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio over the 2015-2020 sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are lower than baseline when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, generating positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative when industrial production rises above its 12-month moving average during stable inflation periods, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and consumer spending expectations, producing immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 2.5% year-over-year forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 50% of months where CPI exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal produces a negative cumulative return over the 2010-2020 period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates corporate profit concentration without consumer support, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of falling unemployment signals a supply-side bottleneck that restricts output growth and generates negative short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where industrial production falls while unemployment declines, or the joint condition fails to predict return direction with a Rank IC above 0.02.
